findings.title: Deployment-time initializer runs before proxy admin is set, enabling privilege capture by the deployer or factory

best_agent_match.title: Deployment-time initializer runs under the deployer/factory, enabling privilege capture

findings.title: `upgradeToAndCall` can strand privileged roles on a non-forwarding transparent-proxy admin

best_agent_match.title: upgradeToAndCall can irreversibly assign ownership to the proxy admin, which is then blocked from using the proxy

findings.title: Constructor accepts `address(0)` as admin, permanently disabling upgrades and admin recovery

best_agent_match.title: Constructor allows `_admin = address(0)`, permanently bricking all admin and upgrade functions

findings.title: Proxy never revalidates implementation code at call time, so a code-less implementation address can turn calls into silent no-ops

best_agent_match.title: Delegation never re-checks code existence, so a vanished implementation can turn proxy calls into silent no-ops

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Initialization Delegatecall in Constructor
rejected_candidates.reason: Generic constructor delegatecall is the intended proxy initialization mechanism. The reportable issue here is the specific admin-ordering hazard captured in F-001, not delegatecall-based storage writes by themselves.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Delegatecall in upgradeToAndCall
rejected_candidates.reason: `upgradeToAndCall` is supposed to delegate into the new implementation for initialization, and only the trusted admin can invoke it. Without the transparent-admin role-stranding condition captured in F-002, this is not a standalone vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Upgrade to Non-Contract Address After Initial Check
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `upgradeTo` calls `_upgradeTo`, which calls `_setImplementation`, and `_setImplementation` explicitly rejects non-contract addresses at `UpgradeabilityProxy.sol:69-75`.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Zero Address Check in changeAdmin
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `changeAdmin` explicitly requires `newAdmin != address(0)` at `AdminUpgradeabilityProxy.sol:77-80`.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Return Value in upgradeToAndCall
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. The code checks the only relevant value, `success`, and initializer return data is intentionally ignored.

rejected_candidates.title: No Initialization Guard in Proxy
rejected_candidates.reason: False as stated. This proxy constructor always sets the implementation and rejects non-contract targets, so the implementation slot cannot remain unset through the provided deployment path.

rejected_candidates.title: isContract Returns False During Constructor
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a documented OpenZeppelin limitation of `extcodesize`, not a codebase-specific exploit in the reviewed proxy flows.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Reentrancy in sendValue
rejected_candidates.reason: `Address.sendValue` is not used by the reviewed contracts, so this is only a generic library warning rather than a vulnerability in this codebase.
